questions.prompt: Why use custom bridge networks over the default bridge?

choices.text: Built-in DNS resolution. Default bridge has no name resolution between containers; custom bridges include embedded DNS so containers reach each other by container name. Docker Compose creates a custom bridge automatically. Use custom bridges for any multi-container setup.

questions.explanation: Custom bridges have DNS; default doesn't.

questions.prompt: Container published with `-p 8080:80` but unreachable from outside. App is listening. Why?

choices.text: App likely listening on `127.0.0.1:80` inside container, not `0.0.0.0:80`. Inside the container, localhost is the container itself. Docker forwards to the container's IP at port 80; an app bound only to loopback doesn't accept it. Bind to 0.0.0.0.

questions.explanation: Apps inside containers must bind to 0.0.0.0, not 127.0.0.1.

questions.prompt: Volume vs bind mount — when which?

choices.text: Volumes for production data (Docker-managed, portable, survives lifecycle). Bind mounts for development source code (host path mounted directly for hot reload). tmpfs for sensitive temp data. Database data directories always volume-mount.

questions.explanation: Volumes prod, bind dev, tmpfs ephemeral.

questions.prompt: Postgres in Docker loses data on restart. Cause?

choices.text: No volume mounted at `/var/lib/postgresql/data`. Without it, data lives in container's writable layer, destroyed on container removal. Mount a named volume there to persist.

questions.explanation: Stateful workloads need volume-mounted data directories.

questions.prompt: What does `depends_on: condition: service_healthy` do that plain `depends_on` doesn't?

choices.text: Waits for the dependency's healthcheck to pass before starting dependents. Plain `depends_on` only orders startup; the dependent can crash because the dependency isn't actually ready. With healthcheck + service_healthy, Compose waits for real readiness. Standard pattern for app + DB stacks.

questions.explanation: service_healthy waits for healthcheck; plain depends_on is order-only.
